medical_history.chief_complaint: Unconscious state with a GCS of 8
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Massive intraventricular hemorrhage with an asymmetrical pattern that is more notable in the atrium of the right lateral ventricle and a remarkable spot sign in the same location

radiographic_examinations.findings: Massive intraventricular hemorrhage with an asymmetrical pattern, more notable in the atrium of the right lateral ventricle and a remarkable spot sign in the same location
A spot sign in the atrium of the right ventricle caused by a 4mm saccular aneurysm originating from LPChA

other_tests.procedure_name: Endovascular embolization with n-butyl-cyanoacrylate (nBCA) glue injection

other_tests.findings: Performed to manage the intraventricular hemorrhage

other_tests.date: During the postoperative treatment in the ICU for 40 days

diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Massive primary intraventricular hemorrhage due to idiopathic Lateral Posterior Choroidal Artery Aneurysm
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was based on CT of the brain revealing massive intraventricular hemorrhage with an asymmetrical pattern more notable in the atrium of the right lateral ventricle and a remarkable spot sign in the same location. CT angiography confirmed a 4mm saccular aneurysm originating from the Lateral Posterior Choroidal Artery (LPChA).
